name: Barbara's Cinnamon Rolls
recipeInstructions:
Makes 48 rolls
1. Butter muffin tins; set aside.
2. Mix together water, yeast and sugar in a small bowl and let proof until foamy, about 10 minutes.
3. Combine milk, butter and salt in a small saucepan. Place over medium heat and bring to boil, stirring occasionally. Pour into a large mixing bowl. Add 1/4 cup sugar and 1 cup flour at a time, mixing thoroughly after each cup of flour is added. Add eggs and mix with dough hook on low speed for 3 to 4 minutes.
4. When the dough has cooled to lukewarm, add yeast and remaining flour. Mix on medium speed for 4 to 5 minutes, scraping bowl several times.
5. Divide dough in half and roll into rectangular sheets 1/4 inch thick. Brush surfaces with melted butter.
6. Combine sugar and cinnamon and sprinkle evenly over dough. Roll up each sheet tightly and lay seam side down. Slice into 1/2-inch rounds and place in muffin tins. Cover lightly and let rise in a warm, draft-free area until doubled in bulk (about 15 minutes).
7. About 15 minutes before rolls are ready to bake, preheat oven to 400°F.
8. Bake rolls for 10 to 12 minutes, until light golden brown.
